recipes.title: Spaghetti alla Carbonara
recipes.description: Roman pasta with guanciale, eggs, Pecorino Romano and black pepper. No cream.
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil and cook the spaghetti until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, cut the guanciale into thick strips and render it in a cold dry pan over medium heat until crisp and golden.
In a bowl, whisk the egg yolks, whole egg, grated Pecorino and a generous amount of black pepper into a thick paste.
Reserve one cup of starchy pasta water, then drain the spaghetti and tip it into the pan with the guanciale off the heat.
Pour in the egg mixture and toss vigorously, adding splashes of pasta water until a glossy, creamy sauce coats every strand.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and a final crack of black pepper.

recipes.title: Lasagna alla Bolognese
recipes.description: Layered pasta with slow-cooked ragù, béchamel and Parmigiano, baked golden.
steps: Sweat the onion, carrot and celery in olive oil until soft, then brown the beef and pork until any liquid evaporates.
Stir in the passata, season, and simmer the ragù gently for at least 90 minutes until rich and thick.
Make the béchamel by melting butter, whisking in flour, then slowly adding warm milk and cooking until silky.
Spread a thin layer of ragù in a baking dish, top with pasta sheets, then ragù, béchamel and Parmigiano.
Repeat the layers until everything is used, finishing with béchamel and a generous shower of cheese.
Bake at 180°C for 35 minutes until bubbling and golden, then rest 10 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Risotto alla Milanese
recipes.description: Creamy saffron risotto from Milan, finished with butter and Parmigiano.
steps: Steep the saffron in a small ladle of hot stock and set aside.
Sweat the shallot in half the butter until translucent, then add the rice and toast for two minutes.
Pour in the wine and stir until absorbed, then begin adding hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly.
After about 15 minutes, stir in the saffron infusion and continue cooking until the rice is al dente.
Off the heat, beat in the remaining cold butter and the Parmigiano vigorously to create the mantecatura.
Cover for two minutes, then serve on warm plates so it spreads in a wave.

recipes.title: Osso Buco alla Milanese
recipes.description: Braised veal shanks with white wine, broth and gremolata.
steps: Tie each veal shank with kitchen twine, season and dust with flour.
Brown the shanks in butter and oil on both sides, then remove from the pan.
Soften the onion, carrot and celery in the same pot, then deglaze with the white wine.
Return the shanks to the pot, add the stock, cover and braise gently for 2 hours until fork tender.
Combine lemon zest, garlic and parsley to make the gremolata.
Serve the shanks with the pan sauce and a generous spoon of gremolata, traditionally alongside risotto alla Milanese.

recipes.title: Tiramisu
recipes.description: Layered dessert of espresso-soaked savoiardi, mascarpone cream and cocoa.
steps: Whisk the yolks with sugar over a bain-marie until pale and ribbon-like.
Fold the mascarpone gently into the yolk mixture until smooth.
Whip the egg whites to firm peaks and fold into the mascarpone cream.
Combine the espresso and Marsala in a shallow dish and dip each ladyfinger briefly so it stays firm.
Layer soaked ladyfingers and cream alternately in a dish, finishing with cream.
Refrigerate at least 6 hours, then dust generously with cocoa powder before serving.

recipes.title: Pizza Margherita
recipes.description: Naples classic: thin crust, San Marzano, fior di latte, basil and olive oil.
steps: Dissolve the yeast in water, mix with flour and salt, and knead 10 minutes until smooth.
Cover and rise at room temperature for 2 hours, then divide into 250 g balls and rest 4 hours more.
Preheat oven and stone to the highest setting, ideally 280°C, for at least 45 minutes.
Stretch each ball gently by hand into a thin disc with a thicker rim.
Top with crushed tomato, torn mozzarella, a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt.
Bake on the stone 6-8 minutes until the crust is blistered, then finish with fresh basil leaves.

recipes.title: Spaghetti alla Bolognese
recipes.description: Long-simmered meat ragù with vegetables, wine and tomato over pasta.
steps: Render the pancetta in oil, then sweat the soffritto of onion, carrot and celery until softened.
Add the beef and brown thoroughly, breaking it up with a spoon.
Pour in the wine and let it reduce, then add the milk and simmer until evaporated.
Stir in the passata, season, and cook uncovered on the lowest heat for 2-3 hours.
Cook the pasta in salted water until al dente and drain.
Toss the pasta with the ragù, finishing with grated Parmigiano.

recipes.title: Cacio e Pepe
recipes.description: Three-ingredient Roman pasta of Pecorino, black pepper and starchy water.
steps: Toast the cracked peppercorns in a dry pan until fragrant.
Cook the pasta in lightly salted water until very al dente.
Reserve plenty of pasta water and transfer the pasta to the pan with the pepper.
Add a ladle of warm pasta water to the Pecorino in a bowl and whisk to a creamy paste.
Off the heat, toss the pasta with the cheese paste, adding water as needed for a glossy emulsion.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and pepper on top.

recipes.title: Saltimbocca alla Romana
recipes.description: Veal escalopes layered with prosciutto and sage, finished with white wine.
steps: Pound the veal escalopes thin between sheets of cling film.
Lay a slice of prosciutto and two sage leaves on each piece and secure with a toothpick.
Dust the veal lightly in flour, shaking off excess.
Sear in foaming butter, prosciutto-side down first, for about a minute per side.
Remove the veal, pour in the wine and reduce to a glossy sauce.
Spoon the sauce over the veal and serve at once.

recipes.title: Caprese Salad
recipes.description: Sliced tomato, mozzarella di bufala, basil, olive oil and salt.
steps: Slice the tomatoes into thick rounds and arrange on a platter.
Tear the mozzarella into pieces and tuck them between the tomato slices.
Scatter generous handfuls of basil leaves over the top.
Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with flaky salt and pepper.
Serve at room temperature with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Minestrone
recipes.description: Hearty Italian vegetable soup with beans, pasta and seasonal greens.
steps: Sweat the onion, carrot and celery in olive oil until soft.
Add the potato and zucchini and cook for a few minutes.
Pour in the passata and stock, drop in the Parmigiano rind, and simmer 30 minutes.
Add the beans and pasta and cook until pasta is al dente.
Season and serve with a drizzle of olive oil and grated Parmigiano.

recipes.title: Panzanella
recipes.description: Tuscan summer salad of stale bread, ripe tomato, onion and basil.
steps: Soak the bread in cold water for a few minutes, then squeeze out the liquid and tear into chunks.
Soak the onion slices in cold water to mellow them, then drain.
Cut the tomatoes into wedges, season and let them release their juices.
Combine bread, tomatoes, cucumber, onion and basil in a bowl.
Dress with vinegar and olive oil, toss and rest 20 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Arancini
recipes.description: Sicilian fried rice balls stuffed with ragù and mozzarella.
steps: Cook the rice in saffron-infused stock until tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in Parmigiano and egg, then spread on a tray and chill until firm.
Take a portion of rice, flatten in your palm, fill with ragù and mozzarella, and seal into a ball.
Coat each ball in flour, beaten egg and breadcrumbs.
Deep fry at 180°C until deep golden and crisp all over.
Drain on paper and serve hot.

recipes.title: Vitello Tonnato
recipes.description: Cold sliced veal under a creamy tuna, caper and anchovy sauce.
steps: Simmer the veal with the carrot, celery and onion in salted water for about 90 minutes until tender.
Cool the veal in its broth, then slice paper-thin.
Blend tuna, anchovies, capers, yolks and lemon, drizzling in oil to make a creamy sauce.
Spoon a layer of sauce on a platter, lay the veal slices on top, then cover with more sauce.
Garnish with extra capers and chill at least an hour before serving.

recipes.title: Panettone
recipes.description: Tall Milanese Christmas bread studded with candied fruit and raisins.
steps: Dissolve yeast in warm milk and combine with flour to form a sponge; rise for 1 hour.
Add sugar, yolks, salt and vanilla, then knead in the butter little by little until silky.
Fold in raisins and candied orange peel and place in a panettone paper mould.
Prove at room temperature 6-8 hours until risen above the rim.
Score a cross on top, dot with butter, and bake at 170°C for 45 minutes.
Hang the panettone upside down to cool to keep its airy structure.

recipes.title: Penne all'Arrabbiata
recipes.description: Spicy Roman pasta with tomato, garlic, chili and parsley.
steps: Warm olive oil with sliced garlic and chili flakes until fragrant.
Add the tomatoes, season, and simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Cook the penne in salted water until al dente.
Toss the pasta in the sauce with a little pasta water for sheen.
Finish with parsley and serve with grated Pecorino if you wish.

recipes.title: Bucatini all'Amatriciana
recipes.description: Roman pasta with guanciale, tomato, Pecorino and a kick of chili.
steps: Render the guanciale strips in a dry pan until crisp, then remove half for garnish.
Deglaze the pan with white wine and let it evaporate.
Add tomatoes and chili and simmer 15 minutes until reduced.
Cook the bucatini until al dente in salted water.
Toss the pasta in the sauce, finish with Pecorino and the reserved guanciale.

recipes.title: Ribollita
recipes.description: Tuscan bean and bread soup, thick enough to stand a spoon in.
steps: Sweat onion, carrot and celery in olive oil for 10 minutes.
Add cavolo nero, passata and stock and simmer 30 minutes.
Stir in the beans and torn pieces of stale bread.
Cook a further 15 minutes until very thick.
Rest overnight if possible, then reheat with a generous drizzle of olive oil.

recipes.title: Pollo alla Cacciatora
recipes.description: Hunter-style braised chicken with tomato, herbs and wine.
steps: Brown the seasoned chicken pieces in olive oil until golden, then set aside.
Sauté the onion, garlic and carrot in the same pan.
Pour in the wine and let it reduce by half.
Add tomatoes, rosemary and chicken back to the pan.
Cover and simmer 45 minutes until the chicken is tender.
Stir in the olives and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Focaccia
recipes.description: Ligurian olive oil flatbread, dimpled and topped with rosemary and salt.
steps: Dissolve yeast in water, then mix with flour, salt and half the oil to a wet dough.
Bulk ferment 90 minutes, folding the dough gently every 30 minutes.
Press the dough into an oiled tray and rest 45 minutes.
Dimple the surface with your fingertips and drench with the remaining olive oil.
Scatter rosemary and flaky salt and bake at 220°C for 20 minutes until golden.
Serve warm, cut into squares.

recipes.title: Pesto alla Genovese
recipes.description: Ligurian sauce of basil, pine nuts, garlic, Parmigiano, Pecorino and olive oil.
steps: Pound garlic and salt in a mortar to a paste.
Add pine nuts and crush, then work in basil leaves a handful at a time.
Stir in both cheeses, then drizzle in the olive oil to a creamy sauce.
Cook the trofie in salted water until al dente.
Toss the pasta with the pesto, loosened with a spoon of pasta water.

recipes.title: Gnocchi al Pomodoro
recipes.description: Pillowy potato gnocchi tossed in fresh tomato sauce with basil.
steps: Boil the potatoes in their skins until tender, then peel and pass through a ricer while warm.
Mix in flour, yolk and salt to a soft dough; do not overwork.
Roll into ropes, cut into pillows, and shape on the back of a fork.
Cook the gnocchi in salted water until they float, about 2 minutes.
Warm crushed tomatoes with garlic and oil, then toss the drained gnocchi in the sauce.
Serve with torn basil and grated Parmigiano.

recipes.title: Bistecca alla Fiorentina
recipes.description: Thick Tuscan T-bone steak grilled rare over wood embers.
steps: Take the steak out of the fridge an hour before cooking and pat dry.
Heat a grill until embers glow white-hot.
Grill the steak 5 minutes per side, then stand it on the bone for 5 minutes more.
Rest the steak under foil 10 minutes; the centre should be rare.
Slice along the bone, season with salt, pepper, oil and rosemary.
Serve with lemon wedges on the side.

recipes.title: Cannoli Siciliani
recipes.description: Crisp fried pastry shells filled with sweet ricotta and candied fruit.
steps: Mix flour, sugar, lard and Marsala into a smooth dough and rest 1 hour.
Roll the dough thin, cut into ovals and wrap around metal cannoli tubes, sealing with egg white.
Fry in hot oil at 180°C until golden and bubbled, then cool on a rack.
Beat the ricotta with icing sugar until smooth, then fold in the candied orange and most of the chocolate chips.
Pipe the filling into both ends of the shells just before serving.
Dust with icing sugar and dot the ends with extra chocolate chips.

recipes.title: Panna Cotta
recipes.description: Silky Piedmontese cooked cream set with gelatin, served with berries.
steps: Soak the gelatin sheets in cold water until soft.
Warm the cream, milk, sugar and split vanilla pod until just below a simmer.
Squeeze out the gelatin and stir into the warm cream until dissolved.
Strain into ramekins and chill at least 4 hours until set.
Unmould onto plates and top with fresh berries.
